text: It's usually best to push through the intercostal muscles with a tonsil clamp, thereby creating a small opening.
It's crucial to make the initial direction of the tunnel parallel to the ribs for four or five centimeters.
At this point, move the tip of the tunnel instrument slightly side to side to increase the width of the tunnel to approximately 1 to 1.5 cm.
The direction of the curved tunneler becomes more cephalad as the dissection progresses.
The tunneler is removed and a 5-inch On-Q Silver Soaker catheter is threaded into the sheath.
Holding the catheter exactly at the level of the split maintains the position of the catheter in the subplural pocket while the sheath is pulled out and peeled apart.
